question: Q-open-composition-kernel-counterexamples-v2
hypothesis: A factory-sealed OpenHSWM with immutable field snapshots, injective mount-qualified interfaces, structured separation receipts, and a safe explicit materializer closes the five frozen-v1 counterexamples without regressing v1 or B0 laws.
falsifiers: Two valid mounts with the same local port ID fail composition.
A connector can address a non-exposed operand port.
Mutating the caller's legacy Field after capture changes canonical or materialized state.
Separation depends on delimiter-concatenated synthetic boundary IDs.
An isolated vertex is lost or a vertex conflict becomes grouping-dependent.
Empty provenance is accepted by snapshot capture.
An overlapping edge collapses without an explicit shared-edge capability.
compose invokes legacy or v2 materialization.
Any frozen v1 or legacy B0 regression fails.
non_claims: No relation compatibility registry is implemented.
No cyclic query readout or learned agent loop is implemented.
No scientific progressive verdict may be inferred from the property tests.
